chief_complaint: A 55-year-old male patient presented with recurrent episodes of gastrointestinal bleeding.
history: The patient has a history of liver cirrhosis and portal hypertension. He underwent a transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt (TIPS) procedure 6 months ago for refractory ascites. However, he recently experienced a TIPS thrombosis, leading to recurrent gastrointestinal bleeding. He has been taking spironolactone and furosemide for ascites management.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed a distended abdomen with shifting dullness.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: Hb 8.5 g/dL, platelets 150 x 10^9/L. Imaging: TIPS thrombosis confirmed on CT angiography.
